title: melica ramosa subsp. major
intro: Melica ramosa subsp. major, also known as the greater wood melick, is a plant species within the grass family (Poaceae), specifically a type of wild grass native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. In herbal preparations, Melica ramosa subsp. major is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
